questions.question: What determines the overall effect when multiple forces act on an object?
questions.answer: The overall effect depends on the net force acting on the object.

questions.question: What is meant by balanced forces?
questions.answer: Balanced forces are equal forces acting in opposite directions that cancel each other.

questions.question: What is the result of balanced forces on an object?
questions.answer: Balanced forces do not change the state of motion of an object.

questions.question: What are unbalanced forces?
questions.answer: Unbalanced forces occur when forces acting on an object are unequal.

questions.question: What effect do unbalanced forces have on objects?
questions.answer: Unbalanced forces change the motion of objects.

questions.question: Why does friction occur between surfaces?
questions.answer: Friction occurs due to irregularities on surfaces in contact.

questions.question: What is rolling friction?
questions.answer: Rolling friction is the resistance that occurs when an object rolls over a surface.

questions.question: Which type of friction is usually the least?
questions.answer: Rolling friction is usually the least.

questions.question: What is electrostatic force?
questions.answer: Electrostatic force is the force between charged objects.

questions.question: Why is gravitational force considered a non-contact force?
questions.answer: Because it acts on objects without physical contact.

questions.question: What is inertia related to?
questions.answer: Inertia is related to the resistance of an object to changes in motion.

questions.question: Why is more force needed to move a heavy object?
questions.answer: Heavy objects have greater inertia.

questions.question: What is the direction of frictional force?
questions.answer: Friction acts opposite to the direction of motion.

questions.question: Why do lubricants reduce friction?
questions.answer: Lubricants fill surface irregularities and reduce contact between surfaces.

questions.question: Why do wheels reduce friction?
questions.answer: Wheels convert sliding friction into rolling friction.

questions.question: What is the net force when two equal forces act in opposite directions?
questions.answer: The net force is zero.

questions.question: Why does a moving bicycle eventually stop if not pedaled?
questions.answer: It stops due to friction and air resistance.

questions.question: What is air resistance?
questions.answer: Air resistance is a type of friction exerted by air on moving objects.

questions.question: Why do skydivers use parachutes?
questions.answer: Parachutes increase air resistance and slow down the fall.

questions.question: Why does a magnet attract iron objects?
questions.answer: Because iron is a magnetic material affected by magnetic force.

questions.question: What determines the strength of gravitational force?
questions.answer: The mass of objects and the distance between them determine gravitational force.

questions.question: Why do rough surfaces produce more friction?
questions.answer: Rough surfaces have greater irregularities that interlock.

questions.question: Why are ball bearings used in machines?
questions.answer: Ball bearings reduce friction by converting sliding friction into rolling friction.

questions.question: What is the relationship between force and motion?
questions.answer: Force can change the speed or direction of motion of an object.

questions.question: Why is understanding forces important in science?
questions.answer: Understanding forces helps explain motion and interactions between objects.
